modals.betaWhat: What it is and what we aim for
modals.betaDescP1: FindYourBet is a social network for sports picks, not just a tracker. Our goal is to make sports picks transparent and social: register your picks, see your real performance with verifiable data, and discover the tipsters who truly win in the long run.
modals.betaDescP2: We want to build a transparent and honest community, where performance is proven with numbers, not promises. Always bet responsibly.
modals.betaHow1: Register your picks in History so your stats (yield, accuracy, streaks) are real.
modals.betaHow2: Follow tipsters and join their channels to see their picks and analysis in real time.
modals.betaHow3: Participate: comment, like, share picks by DM and compete in the rankings.
modals.betaHow4: If you're a tipster, create your channel and share your methodology with the community.
modals.betaInc1: Pick registration with full stats and history
modals.betaInc2: Public channels with chat, picks and polls
modals.betaInc5: Profiles, follows, direct messages and community
modals.betaSoon1: Private channels (by link, paid VIP and single-pick) — not yet available
modals.betaSoon4: Full ranking system with tiers and 0–100 score
modals.betaSoon5: Public tipster profiles optimised for sharing
modals.betaSoon6: Integrated usage guides for each feature
modals.betaFooter: FindYourBet is in public beta. Some things may change or improve. Your feedback helps us — write to us from Contact / Suggestions.

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions
faqs.subtitle: Everything you need to know about FindYourBet and the world of tipsters.
faqs.contactFooter: Can't find what you're looking for? Write to us at fyourbet@gmail.com and we'll reply within 24h.
faqs.a1_1: FindYourBet (FYB) is a social network for sports picks. Tipsters create channels where they share their picks and analysis in real time. Users follow the best tipsters, learn from their methodology and improve their results with real, verifiable data.
faqs.a1_2: We want to make the world of sports picks more transparent and meritocratic. Every tipster has a public, unalterable history of all their picks. No manipulated screenshots or made-up results: only real data. We believe transparency is the only way to separate honest tipsters from those who aren't.
faqs.a1_3: Yes. Signing up, following tipsters and accessing public channels is completely free. Tipsters can create paid VIP channels with exclusive analysis — this feature is coming soon.
faqs.a2_1: Yield measures a tipster's profitability relative to their total stake. It's calculated as: (Net profit ÷ Sum of stakes) × 100. A positive Yield is already a good sign. Above 5% is excellent. The most important thing is that it's consistent over time, not just in a single period.
faqs.q2_2: What is the Bank and how does it work in FYB?
faqs.a2_2: The Bank is the total capital allocated to picks. In FYB we use a management system based on 1% of the bank per pick: if your bank is €1,000, each stake is €10. As the bank grows or shrinks, the stake adjusts proportionally. This system protects your capital during bad runs and maximises growth in good times.
faqs.q2_3: What is the average odds and why does it matter?
faqs.a2_3: The average odds is the mean of all pick odds. Higher odds imply greater variance — meaning more ups and downs in the bankroll even if the Yield is positive. A tipster with average odds of 1.60 will be more stable than one at 3.50, even if both have the same Yield.
faqs.a2_4: The Ranking classifies tipsters by their Yield over the last 3 months. To appear, a tipster needs a minimum number of picks registered in that period. The more picks with a sustained positive Yield, the higher on the list. The goal is to reward consistency, not lucky streaks.
faqs.q3_1: How to avoid being scammed by a tipster?
faqs.a3_1: Apply these golden rules: (1) Be wary of anyone promising guaranteed results — no one wins all the time. (2) Check that the history is extensive: one month of good picks means nothing statistically. (3) Look at long-term Yield, not just the latest period. (4) In FYB, picks are registered with date and time before the match, guaranteeing they're not invented after the fact.
faqs.q3_2: What does FYB guarantee in terms of transparency?
faqs.a3_2: In FYB, every pick is recorded with its exact timestamp. This prevents tipsters from publishing picks "after the fact" — meaning announcing winning picks they didn't actually register before the match. The history is public, verifiable and cannot be edited once resolved.
faqs.q4_1: How to find the best tipster for me?
faqs.a4_1: Follow these criteria: (1) Sustained positive Yield for at least 3 months. (2) High number of picks — the more picks, the more statistically significant the Yield. (3) Average odds suited to your risk tolerance. (4) Detailed analysis in picks: a good tipster reasons, they don't just announce. (5) Picks in sports and markets you also understand.
faqs.q4_2: How many picks does a tipster need to be reliable?
faqs.a4_2: At minimum, 50–100 resolved picks are needed for the Yield to start being statistically relevant. With fewer than 30, results can be pure chance. The larger the history, the more confidence you can place in the performance shown.
faqs.q4_3: Should I follow one tipster or several?
faqs.a4_3: Diversifying across 2–4 tipsters with different styles (sports, odds, markets) reduces the overall variance of your bankroll. Avoid following too many at once, as you lose control over your total stakes. The key is quality over quantity.
